agency: NYC Office of Administrative Trials & Hearings (OATH)
method: Outside-in crawl (browser UA, robots-respecting — nyc.gov robots.txt only disallows /html/misc/). Fingerprinted the informational site nyc.gov/site/oath (Akamai + nginx + NYC.gov 'Livesite' platform v22 + Dynatrace) and the ECB Ticket Finder / Respond-to-a-Summons portal at a820-ecbticketfinder.nyc.gov/searchHome.action, identified as an Apache Struts 2 application on Oracle WebLogic (.action routing, X-ORACLE-DMS-ECID header, WebLogic '!' JSESSIONID, ISO-8859-1). Verified the NYC Open Data agency label 'Office of Administrative Trials and Hearings (OATH)' via the Socrata Discovery API and pulled both assets with column schemas.

meta.platform: NYC.gov Livesite (Akamai/nginx/Dynatrace) informational site + a legacy Apache Struts / Oracle WebLogic ECB Ticket Finder portal
meta.netnew: SummonsDispute — respond to / dispute a summons (request a hearing) via an owned API
meta.tagline: OATH publishes every ECB summons, hearing, and decision as open data, but the one thing a respondent needs to do — respond to a summons — is trapped in a legacy Struts portal with no API.
meta.headline: The adjudication data is wide open; the act of responding to a summons is locked in an Apache Struts / WebLogic portal.

scorecard_notes.platform: shared NYC.gov Livesite CMS + a dated Apache Struts / Oracle WebLogic transaction portal
scorecard_notes.siteApi: no content or site API; HTML only
scorecard_notes.openData: only 2 datasets, but the flagship Hearings Division Case Status is huge and updated daily
scorecard_notes.coreData: case-status data is exceptionally rich and machine-readable — every summons, charge, hearing, decision, penalty, and balance
scorecard_notes.writeApi: responding to / disputing a summons has no API — Struts portal, mail, or in person only
scorecard_notes.agent: no agent-native surface of any kind
scorecard_notes.ownership: data on Tyler/Socrata; the respond-to-summons transaction on a vendor Struts/WebLogic stack

headline_findings: OATH is a split domain: an informational NYC.gov site (Akamai/nginx/Livesite v22/Dynatrace) plus the ECB Ticket Finder / Respond-to-a-Summons portal (a820-ecbticketfinder.nyc.gov), a legacy Apache Struts 2 app on Oracle WebLogic (.action, X-ORACLE-DMS-ECID, WebLogic JSESSIONID) with no API.

The adjudication data is exceptionally OPEN: the 'OATH Hearings Division Case Status' dataset (jz4z-kudi, 74 columns, updated DAILY, ~400k lifetime views) publishes every ECB summons — charges, respondent, violation location, hearing, decision, penalty, and balance — plus 'OATH Trials Division Case Status' (y3hw-z6bm) for the tribunal side.

But the respondent SERVICE layer has no machine-readable surface: looking up and RESPONDING to a summons — dispute it, request or reschedule a hearing, submit a defense, reopen a default — is only possible through the legacy Struts portal, by mail, or in person.
OATH adjudicates but does not issue: the summonses come from DOB, DSNY, FDNY, DOHMH, DEP, DCWP and others; unpaid decisions become docketed judgments collected by the Department of Finance.
Net-new write surface: SummonsDispute — respond to / dispute a summons (request a hearing) — the single most common respondent transaction, with no API today. See tech-stack.md and apis-observed.md.

apis_observed.note: 2 OATH datasets — Hearings Division Case Status (jz4z-kudi, 74c, daily) and Trials Division Case Status (y3hw-z6bm). SODA /resource/<id>.json endpoints. The one real, open OATH API — case-status data only.

Look up a summons by ticket number or respondent and respond/dispute it. Server-rendered Struts 2 (.action), Oracle WebLogic (X-ORACLE-DMS-ECID, JSESSIONID '!'), ISO-8859-1; no JSON/OpenAPI. search.action + getViolationbyID.action; form fields searchViolationObject.violationNo / respondentName / borough.
content only — hearings, defaults, payments/penalties, reopen-a-default. Akamai edge, nginx, Dynatrace RUM. No content API.

fruit.name: ECB Summonses (Hearings Division Case Status)
opendata_match: OATH Hearings Division Case Status jz4z-kudi (74c, daily)
fruit.notes: The core object. Every ECB summons with up to ten charges (code/section/description/amount), respondent, violation location (with Borough/Block/Lot), issuing agency, hearing, decision, penalty, and balance. Keyed on Ticket Number. OATH's most-viewed dataset (~400k).

fruit.name: Hearings & Defaults
opendata_match: OATH Hearings Division Case Status jz4z-kudi (Hearing Date/Time, Hearing Status, Scheduled Hearing Location, Hearing Result)
fruit.notes: Hearing schedule, status, and result carried inside the Hearings Division dataset; the informational page explains in-person/phone/online hearings and what a default means.

fruit.name: Decisions, Penalties & Payments
opendata_match: OATH Hearings Division Case Status jz4z-kudi (Hearing Result, Decision Date, Penalty Imposed, Additional Penalties or Late Fees, Date Judgment Docketed, Balance Due, Paid Amount)
fruit.notes: The disposition, penalty, and docketed-judgment fields are open; actual payment is handed off to the Department of Finance and has no OATH API.

fruit.name: OATH Trials Division Cases
opendata_match: OATH Trials Division Case Status y3hw-z6bm (16c, monthly)
fruit.notes: The tribunal side — agency disciplinary/licensing matters: Case Number, Type, Category, Filing Agency Case ID, conference/trial/report dates, dispo code, agency head decision, appeal.

fruit.name: Look up a summons (ECB Ticket Finder)
fruit.notes: Search a summons by ticket number or respondent name/address. Legacy Apache Struts / Oracle WebLogic UI; no API (the case-status data exists on Open Data, but this respondent-facing lookup does not).

fruit.name: Respond to / dispute a summons (request a hearing)
fruit.notes: The single most common respondent transaction — dispute a summons, request or reschedule a hearing, submit a written defense. No API and no Open Data twin; lives only in the Struts portal, by mail, or in person. The net-new WRITE surface for this domain.

fruit.name: Reopen a missed hearing (default) online
fruit.notes: Ask OATH to reopen a defaulted summons and grant a new hearing — a variant of the SummonsDispute write surface (requestType = Reopen Default). Portal-only; no API.

fruit.name: Criminal Justice Reform Act (CJRA) Quarterly Report, Vol. 9 No. 4
fruit.notes: CJRA quarterly statistics on criminal-to-civil summons diversion; the only Excel on the OATH site.
